summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates installer environment via obfuscated postinstall + hand-rolled TLS on import
details: Package executes a two-stage installer-data capture and exfiltration chain. At install time, the postinstall script install.js reads process.env in bulk (accessing 'process' and 'env' via XOR-obfuscated string helpers), filters only keys containing 'path', and writes the remainder along with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and cwd to /tmp/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. Credential-shaped variables such as AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, and NPM_TOKEN survive the filter. On module load, dist/index.js (the declared main) reads that marker file, XORs the contents with the repeating key 'electron-orbit', and hex-encodes the result. It then opens a raw node:net socket to port 443 on a hostname assembled from XOR-obfuscated fragments that decode to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net, performing a hand-rolled ECDHE-P256 + AES-128-GCM TLS 1.3 handshake (buildClientHello, encryptRecord/decryptRecord) rather than using node:https or node:tls — bypassing Node's TLS stack, HTTP proxies, and EDR/DPI inspection. Activation of the fetch URL is gated by a kill-switch: the target is populated only when sha256(process.env.BuildType) is a substring of a hardcoded 64-char hex constant, otherwise set to '%TEMP%', letting the operator keep the payload dormant on sandboxes while shipping the full harvest+encode+transmit chain to every installer. Package name and keywords target the Electron ecosystem while author metadata points at the unrelated OrbitDB archive, consistent with namespace-abuse impersonation to draw installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
